title: Avoid duplicate conditions in if-else chains
description: Duplicate conditions in if-else chains lead to dead code since only the first occurrence will ever be executed. This is usually caused by copy-paste errors and can hide intended logic or create unexpected behavior.
recommendation: Remove duplicate conditions or correct the logic to use the intended condition.

noncompliant.description: Duplicate conditions create unreachable code and indicate logic errors.

compliant.description: Ensure each condition is unique and arrange them logically to avoid unreachable code.

checks.message: Duplicate condition detected in if-else chain: This condition will never be reached as it's identical to a previous condition
